mnemonics.title: Remember Stack vs Heap
mnemonics.content: S.A.D. - Stack Allocates Downward, Heap Allocates Dynamically

mnemonics.title: Remember delete vs delete[]
mnemonics.content: A for Array - delete[] for arrays, single delete for single objects

acronyms.usage: Binds resource lifetime to object lifetime, used by smart pointers for automatic memory management
Paradigm where memory management is handled through classes and objects
C++ library containing containers that use dynamic memory internally

visualTips.visualization: Draw a vertical memory diagram. Stack grows downward from top, Heap grows upward from bottom. Stack is like stacked plates (LIFO), Heap is like scattered objects in a parking lot.
Imagine a leaky faucet - water (memory) keeps dripping (being allocated) but the bucket (heap) never gets emptied (freed). Eventually the bucket overflows (program crashes).
Draw a house (memory block), show address written on paper (pointer), then demolish house but keep the paper - that's a dangling pointer pointing to nothing valid.

analogies.analogy: Static allocation is like reserving a specific seat in a theater - the seat size and location are fixed and known in advance.
Dynamic allocation is like renting a hotel room - you request a room size you need at check-in (runtime), and you must check out (deallocate) when done.
A memory leak is like losing your car keys and buying new ones instead of looking for them - you keep consuming resources without releasing the old ones.
Smart pointers are like having a responsible friend who automatically returns your book when you're done reading - they handle cleanup automatically.
